response.justification: Bogdan is a highly versatile Staff-level engineer with 15+ years of broad software delivery experience and recent hands-on work building MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure — directly relevant to this role's key technologies. However, Python is entirely absent from his skill set despite being a primary language requirement, and there is no evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM performance tuning, or multi-agent frameworks such as LangGraph or CrewAI. His AI-specific work is confined to his current role starting April 2025, falling significantly short of the 8-year AI development depth the job requires. The forward-deployed, customer-facing consulting dimension is also not demonstrated. A conversation could clarify whether his AI exposure runs deeper than the resume suggests.
strengths: Direct MCP (Model Context Protocol) framework experience aligns precisely with a listed key technology
Staff-level track record across distributed systems, AWS CDK, data integration, and developer tooling
Strong software craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based dev) relevant to building production-grade agentic systems
concerns: Python is absent from the skills list; virtually all AI/ML frameworks and pipelines in the JD are Python-first
No evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM optimization, or multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI)
AI and agentic work is limited to ~1 month in the current role; the 8-year AI-driven development requirement is not met

key_factors.factor: AI experience depth (<1 year vs 8 required)
MCP and agentic workflow hands-on experience
